seo.title: Cloudflare Workers vs Cloudflare Pages vs Vercel Edge Fun...
seo.description: A practical comparison of Cloudflare Workers, Cloudflare Pages, and Vercel Edge Functions for Edge Deployment.

content.intro: Compare edge deployment platforms focusing on implementation effort, lock-in risk, and performance trade-offs for latency-sensitive workloads.

options.name: Cloudflare Workers
options.tagline: Low-latency serverless execution with global edge network
options.best_for: Global latency-sensitive applications requiring minimal tail latency

options.name: Vercel Edge Functions
options.tagline: Seamless integration with Next.js and modern web apps
options.best_for: Full-stack developers using React/Next.js with regional execution needs

options.name: Deno Deploy
options.tagline: Modern runtime with first-class TypeScript and security

values.Deno Deploy: Full TypeScript/JavaScript runtime with secure modules

criteria.name: Global Edge Network
criteria.description: Latency reduction through distributed edge infrastructure

values.Deno Deploy: Edge locations in 30+ regions with 150ms median latency

criteria.name: Database Integration
criteria.description: Edge database access patterns and limitations

values.Cloudflare Workers: D1 SQL, Turso, PlanetScale with replication
values.Deno Deploy: Deno KV, PostgreSQL with async drivers

criteria.name: Vendor Lock-in Risk
criteria.description: Ease of migration between edge platforms

criteria.name: Performance Consistency
criteria.description: Reliability under peak load and cold starts

verdict.summary: Cloudflare Workers excels in global latency reduction but requires careful API management. Vercel Edge Functions offers tight Next.js integration with moderate lock-in. Deno Deploy provides open-source flexibility with higher cold start overhead.

use_cases.reason: Optimized for 195+ edge locations with minimal tail latency
Low lock-in with first-class TypeScript and secure module system
